thread.text: Where am I losing the most money right now?
Your biggest leak is at checkout — it takes 2.4s longer than it should, and that's costing you **−$18k to $42k per month** in lost sales. I've created a P1 action to fix this.
What broke since the last analysis cycle?
3 regressions detected in the last 14 days: checkout abandonment **+12%**, payment validation failed on 2 routes, SSL cert expires in 5 days. The first two are already in the queue at P1 and P3.

maps_panel.subtext: Main conversion path and where visitors drop off.

overlay_ai.message: I scanned 12 surfaces and found 23 findings. The top 3 are critical and account for 64% of the estimated loss.

guided.step2_ai:
Just browsed your checkout. Payment stalls at **4.2s** — three scripts load before the form appears, and **12% of your users** are leaving at this point. That's **−$600/day** in revenue.

Monthly cost: **−$28k**.

Already prioritized the fix as P1 in your queue. Defer the scripts, compress checkout images, and move validation to async. Let me know when it's live — I'll run through again to confirm.

Created a map for you to monitor user behavior.

items.question: What does Vestigio do?
items.answer: Vestigio analyzes your site and shows you exactly where you're losing money — from the ad click to the purchase. It finds the problems, calculates the impact in dollars, and tells you what to fix first.

items.question: How fast will I see results?
items.answer: Your first diagnostic is ready in 60 seconds. Clients who apply the priority fixes typically see impact within the first week.

items.question: Do I need a developer to set it up?
items.answer: No. Just paste your site URL. Vestigio does everything else — no code, no tags, no technical integration needed to start.

items.question: Is it free? What does it cost after?
items.answer: The first diagnostic is 100% free, no credit card required. After that, you can choose a plan with continuous monitoring and AI-prioritized actions.

items.question: Does it work with my platform?
items.answer: Yes. Vestigio works with any website — Shopify, WooCommerce, Nuvemshop, WordPress, custom sites, and landing pages. If it has a URL, we can analyze it.

solution_layers.title: More traffic won't fix it. The money leaks between the click and the conversion.

cards.title: You pay for the click. But between click and purchase, there's a hole.
cards.body: Between the click and the purchase, **up to 67% of visitors vanish**. You're paying for every one of them.

cards.title: You try to fix it, but the problem just moves.
cards.body: You change the ad, the drop-off **moves to another page**. Without end-to-end visibility, every fix creates a new problem.

cards.title: Nobody complains. The money just stops coming in.

playbook_prompts.revenue_leak_full_audit: Run a complete revenue leak analysis. Show me every finding that causes revenue loss, ranked by monthly dollar impact from highest to lowest. For the top 5, explain the root cause and what fixing each one would recover. Then show the total combined monthly loss and the estimated recovery if I fix the top 3.
playbook_prompts.conversion_bottleneck: Map my conversion funnel from landing to purchase/signup. At each stage, show which findings create friction. Where is the biggest single drop-off? What's the root cause of that drop-off? If I could only fix one thing to improve conversion, what should it be and how much would it move the needle?
playbook_prompts.trust_signal_audit: Score my site's trust signals across these dimensions: SSL & security badges, reviews & testimonials, company information (about, team, address), contact options (phone, chat, email), policies (returns, privacy, terms), payment trust (logos, guarantees), social proof (customer count, media mentions). For each dimension, rate 1-10 and explain what's missing. What's my overall trust score and what would improve it fastest?
playbook_prompts.executive_summary: Create an executive summary suitable for a board meeting. Include: total monthly revenue at risk (sum of all findings), top 3 critical issues with dollar impact, 90-day priority roadmap (week 1-2: quick wins, month 1: critical fixes, month 2-3: strategic improvements), expected ROI of the full remediation plan, and a single health score from 0-100 for the business.
playbook_prompts.cross_pack_correlation: Analyze findings across ALL packs (revenue integrity, scale readiness, chargeback resilience). Find correlations: Which findings from different packs share the same root cause? Where does fixing a revenue issue also reduce chargeback risk? Where does improving trust also help conversion? Build a correlation map showing connected findings across packs with combined impact.

root_cause_descriptions.trust_failure_at_checkout: Buyers reach the moment of payment but trust signals around them weaken instead of strengthen. Off-domain handoffs, unrecognized payment providers, and missing security cues at the wrong moment cause buyers to back out — exactly when conversion is closest. The buyer wanted to pay; the experience scared them off.
root_cause_descriptions.fragmented_conversion_path: Your purchase flow is split across multiple domains or requires too many steps. Every transition between surfaces is a fresh chance for the buyer to drop. The path that should feel like one continuous experience feels like a relay race with handoffs at every step.
root_cause_descriptions.friction_barrier_on_path: Technical obstacles on the path from intent to purchase — slow pages, broken or excessively long forms, redundant redirects — directly suppress conversion. Buyers don't push through friction; they bounce. Every step that asks too much is a step that loses revenue.
root_cause_descriptions.measurement_blindspot: The pages that generate revenue aren't being measured. Without analytics on the commerce path, every other improvement is guesswork — you can't optimize what you can't see. Decisions get made on intuition instead of data, and the leaks stay invisible.
root_cause_descriptions.policy_deficiency: Required consumer policies (privacy, terms, returns) are missing, incomplete, or buried where buyers can't find them. This creates legal risk, breaks trust at the purchase moment, and blocks paid traffic acceptance on platforms that require policy presence before serving ads.
root_cause_descriptions.active_revenue_leakage: Revenue is escaping right now through specific broken paths — dead checkout flows, broken forms, missing conversion routes. This isn't a hypothetical risk; it's money leaving today. Every cycle this remains is direct revenue loss with a quantifiable dollar tag.
root_cause_descriptions.weak_conversion_signal: Visitors land on your commercial pages but cannot see how to buy from you. The primary call-to-action is missing, unclear, or competing with louder secondary actions. Even buyers who arrived ready to convert can't find the path — and they leave.
root_cause_descriptions.support_gap: Customers can't reach you when something goes wrong. Hidden contact methods, missing chat, no phone or email near the purchase moment — buyers who can't reach support file disputes instead. Every chargeback is a failed conversation that should have been a support ticket.
root_cause_descriptions.expectation_failure: Buyers complete the purchase without knowing exactly what they bought, when it ships, or how to return it. The mismatch between expectation and reality is the precondition for chargebacks. Confirmation, transparency, and brand continuity at checkout aren't optional — they're the contract that prevents disputes.
root_cause_descriptions.dispute_defenses_absent: The basics that stop buyers from filing chargebacks are missing. Your return policy is unclear, support is hard to reach, buyers don't know exactly what they bought, and the payment page doesn't even look like your brand. Any one of these on its own causes disputes. You have all of them at once — and every dispute costs you money plus a fee.
root_cause_descriptions.channel_integrity_compromise: Outside scripts are running on your payment pages, and your forms send data to places you don't recognize. This opens the door for attackers to steal buyer information or redirect payments. Your store's safety depends on code you don't fully control — and your buyers pay the price if anything breaks.
root_cause_descriptions.commerce_operations_exposed: The admin pages, setup files, and internal tools you use to run your store are sitting out in the open where anyone can find them. Someone who stumbles on them can change your prices, break your checkout, or copy out your customer data. You think these tools are private. They're not.
root_cause_descriptions.weak_channel_posture: When buyers land on your site, their browser flags it as unsafe — missing padlock, security warnings, or "Not Secure" labels. Cautious buyers leave before they even read your offer. Their browser is telling them something is wrong, and they trust the browser more than they trust you.
root_cause_descriptions.commerce_abuse_exposure: Automated scripts are exploiting your discount codes, refund flow, and pricing rules to take money out of your business. They guess coupon codes, trigger fake refunds, and do it faster than you can notice. By the time it shows up in your numbers, you've already paid for it.
root_cause_descriptions.untracked_purchase_paths: Some of your buyers are paying through checkout paths you didn't intend to track or protect. You can't see the revenue they bring in, you can't tell if they're being abused, and you may not even know they exist. Money is moving through your store in places you're not watching.
root_cause_descriptions.runtime_commerce_fragility: Your store works when you test it, but breaks when real buyers open it. Checkout pages load too slowly. Landing pages stall. Buyers reach "Pay" before the button is ready and click nothing. These failures only show up for real buyers on real devices — and every one of them is a sale you lost without knowing why.
root_cause_descriptions.third_party_dependency_risk: Critical buyer experience — payment, trust badges, support widgets, measurement — depends on external services that fail, load late, or add too much weight. When a third party hiccups, your conversion path breaks with it. Reliability you don't control is reliability you can't promise to your buyer.
root_cause_descriptions.saas_activation_barrier: New trial users can't reach the moment where the product proves its value. High-complexity onboarding, missing next-step guidance, or a disconnect between landing promise and in-app reality kills trial-to-paid conversion before users see what they're paying for. They sign up curious and leave unconvinced.
root_cause_descriptions.saas_product_experience_gap: Once inside the product, users can't discover what it does for them. Empty states without guidance, buried features, and complex navigation prevent the activation moment. The product is capable; the experience hides that capability. Users churn for products that aren't actually better — just better at showing their value.
root_cause_descriptions.saas_expansion_blocked: Users who would happily pay for more cannot find the upgrade path or evaluate it. Hidden pricing, missing upgrade CTAs, or prompts shown without value context cap revenue at the current tier even when willingness-to-pay is higher. Money is leaving on the table because the path to give it to you isn't visible.
root_cause_descriptions.brand_inconsistent_in_previews: When your business shows up in Google, on social media, or inside AI assistants like ChatGPT, it looks different every time — and often weak. Missing titles, thin descriptions, and mismatched details shape buyers' first impression before they ever visit your site. You lose trust before you get a chance to earn it.
root_cause_descriptions.commerce_pages_invisible_to_search: Google and AI assistants like ChatGPT can't properly see your product and pricing pages. They're missing the signals search engines look for, so when a buyer searches for exactly what you sell, your competitors show up and you don't. Every search you don't appear in is a buyer who was ready to pay — and found someone else.
root_cause_descriptions.brand_impersonation_exposure: Your brand is being actively impersonated. Lookalike domains, typosquats, phishing surfaces, or fragmented brand variants intercept traffic, run fraudulent transactions, or capture mistyped visits. Every intercepted buyer is direct revenue you earned but never saw — and every fraudulent transaction damages the brand trust you spent years building.
root_cause_descriptions.behavioral_hesitation_at_commitment: Your buyers get to the payment step, then stop. They click around looking for something that will make them feel okay about paying — then they leave. They came to buy. Something made them doubt at the last moment. You can see it happen, and you can fix it.
root_cause_descriptions.behavioral_path_disconnection: Your checkout looks fine when you walk through it, but real buyers get stuck. They land on steps and never move forward. They see the "Buy" button but never click it. Mobile buyers hit dead ends. You only see it when you watch what actual visitors do — which is exactly what Vestigio is doing.
root_cause_descriptions.behavioral_value_justification_gap: Buyers look at your price, then bounce back to your features page, then to pricing again, then leave. They're trying to decide if it's worth it — and nothing on the page tells them clearly enough. They didn't leave because it costs too much. They left because you never made the case that it's worth the cost.
root_cause_descriptions.behavioral_trust_failure_at_input: Your forms ask buyers for card numbers, personal details, and contact info — but nothing on the page reassures them it's safe to share. They reach the field, hesitate, and walk away instead of typing. The moment you ask for something sensitive, buyers need to feel safe. Right now they don't.
root_cause_descriptions.security_posture_inadequate: Buyers see warning signs the moment they land on your site. The padlock is gone, the browser says "Not Secure," and payment forms quietly stop working. Buyers click "Pay" and nothing happens. At the same time, internal admin pages are sitting in the open, one step away from a full breach. This is costing you sales every single day — and setting you up for a much bigger disaster.
root_cause_descriptions.behavioral_first_session_failure: First-time visitors arrive with intent but stall before reaching any revenue milestone. Session data shows new users hitting trust barriers, missing CTAs, or encountering dead-end flows within their first interaction. The first session is the highest-leverage conversion window — when it fails, the buyer rarely returns. Every first-session stall is a permanent loss of the cheapest conversion you'll ever get.
root_cause_descriptions.behavioral_action_value_misalignment: Visitors are busy clicking around your site — just not on the things that lead to a sale. The pages that make you money get the least attention. Your buyers' eyes are in one place, and your "Buy" button is in another. You need to move the sale to where the attention already is.
root_cause_descriptions.paid_acquisition_waste: Every time someone clicks your ad, you pay. But your landing pages are slow, the trust cues are missing, and mobile makes it worse — so most of those expensive visitors never buy. You're paying full price for half the result, and the more you spend on ads, the more you waste.
root_cause_descriptions.mobile_conversion_failure: Mobile visitors encounter conversion barriers that desktop users never see — broken CTAs, form friction, timing gaps between intent and action. The mobile experience systematically underperforms against the same offer, same traffic quality, same buyer intent. Since mobile is typically the majority of traffic, this gap represents the single largest segment-level revenue loss most sites carry.
root_cause_descriptions.behavioral_friction_tax: No single page on your site is broken — but buyers have to work too hard at every step. They go back and forth between pages, re-enter checkout, and get tired of deciding. The effort adds up until they give up. Each page is fine on its own; together they ask more of buyers than they're willing to give.
root_cause_descriptions.behavioral_trust_revenue_gap: Your buyers are ready to pay — then they check your returns policy, look for a support contact, scan for trust badges, and leave anyway. They weren't hunting for reasons to say no. They were looking for one more reason to say yes, and they didn't find it.
root_cause_descriptions.behavioral_path_inefficiency: Buyers arrive ready to buy, but your site makes them walk through too many pages before they can. By the time they reach "Pay," the interest that brought them has faded. Shorter paths turn intent into sales. Longer paths let it slip away.
root_cause_descriptions.copy_strategy_gap: The words on your site — testimonials, error messages, onboarding steps, buttons — aren't saying what buyers actually need to hear. Your testimonials are generic. Your form errors read like computer code. Your onboarding never gives the new user a quick win. At every moment, buyers have a real question in their head, and your copy answers the wrong one.
root_cause_descriptions.vertical_fashion_purchase_barriers: Fashion buyers need to feel confident the product will fit and look right before they spend. Without size guidance, enough photos to judge the piece, and a visible return policy on the product page, they assume the worst and leave. The ones who buy anyway return at 3x the rate — costing you triple shipping on every uncertain purchase.
root_cause_descriptions.vertical_saas_evaluation_friction: Software buyers evaluate before they commit budget. When they can't try the product, can't see it works with their existing tools, and can't tell if it's actively maintained, they move on to a competitor who makes evaluation easy. The sale doesn't go to the best product — it goes to the one that removes doubt fastest.
root_cause_descriptions.vertical_food_ordering_friction: Hungry customers make fast decisions. If they can't see the menu instantly, can't tell whether you deliver to them, or can't estimate when food arrives, they order from the next restaurant in the list. Every extra second of uncertainty is an order lost to a competitor who answers those questions upfront.
root_cause_descriptions.vertical_health_trust_deficit: Health and beauty buyers put products on or in their bodies. Without visible ingredients, clinical backing, and proof of real results from real people, the perceived risk exceeds the purchase price. They won't gamble their health on a product page that doesn't prove safety and efficacy.
root_cause_descriptions.vertical_education_commitment_anxiety: Education buyers commit time, not just money. When they can't see the curriculum, don't know how long it takes, can't preview the content quality, and aren't sure they'll get recognized credentials, the perceived risk of wasted months outweighs the course price. They need proof of outcome before they'll commit.
root_cause_descriptions.vertical_b2b_internal_justification: B2B buyers don't decide alone — they have to convince their boss, their finance team, or a procurement committee. Without case studies with real metrics, a clear methodology, and signals that you serve companies their size, they can't build the internal business case. The sale dies in a Slack thread because your buyer had no ammunition.
root_cause_descriptions.first_impression_failure: Visitors land on your homepage and can't tell what you do, who it's for, or why they should care — within the 5 seconds that determine whether they stay or leave. The headline talks about what you built, not what the buyer gets. The screen is packed with competing messages. The first action is buried below the fold. And your copy sounds like every other competitor in the market. Every visitor who leaves in the first 10 seconds is someone you paid to bring but failed to hold. The first impression is the most expensive failure because it happens to 100% of your traffic.
root_cause_descriptions.consideration_friction: Visitors who survived the first impression try to learn more — but the experience works against them. There's no evidence that real customers exist. Support pages are dead ends with no path back to buying. Features are listed without explaining what they mean for the buyer. Nothing positions your product against alternatives. And your FAQ answers the questions you want to answer, not the ones buyers are actually asking. Each barrier erodes the confidence your first impression built.
root_cause_descriptions.decision_moment_anxiety: Buyers arrive at the pricing page or checkout with intent to pay — and everything amplifies their doubt instead of reducing it. Prices appear without ROI context, so buyers see cost instead of investment. The visual identity breaks at the payment moment. Accepted payment methods aren't visible. Your guarantee exists but is hidden when buyers need it most. And nothing tells the buyer why acting today is better than tomorrow.
root_cause_descriptions.post_purchase_abandonment: New customers complete the purchase but can't find value fast enough to justify staying. There's no clear path to their first result. The support page exists but doesn't promise response time. And no page explains billing cycle, cancellation, or data retention. The gap between paying and getting value is where retention dies.
root_cause_descriptions.expansion_ceiling: Your revenue depends entirely on bringing new customers because existing ones have no way to spend more. Higher plan features are listed without explaining their business value. No mechanism exists for satisfied customers to bring others. And customer success stories happen but are never captured as social proof. Without expansion revenue, your business runs on a treadmill — running faster just to stay in place.

strong_cta_clarity.title: Customers know exactly how to buy
strong_cta_clarity.description: Your buy button is clear and there is no competing call-to-action stealing attention. Visitors do not have to think twice about how to purchase.

trust_continuity_good.title: Customers stay on your domain through checkout
trust_continuity_good.description: The buyer never gets bounced to a strange domain during payment. The whole flow happens where they expect it — no surprise redirects, no broken trust.

policy_coverage_complete.title: Your policies are in place and easy to find
policy_coverage_complete.description: Privacy, terms, and refund policies are visible and accessible. Buyers can find what they need before clicking buy — and that builds trust.

low_friction_checkout.title: Your purchase path runs clean
low_friction_checkout.description: No broken forms, no redirect chains, no slow pages between cart and confirmation. The buyer who wants to buy can actually buy.

measurement_covered.title: You can see what is happening in your funnel
measurement_covered.description: Analytics are in place across the buying path. You can track where buyers come from, where they convert, and where they drop off.

support_reachable.title: Customers can find you when they have a problem
support_reachable.description: Contact options are visible and reachable. When something goes wrong, the buyer talks to you instead of opening a chargeback.

smooth_activation.title: New users can get to value fast
smooth_activation.description: Your onboarding shows clear next steps and does not pile complexity on day one. Users get to the "aha" moment without getting stuck.

navigation_clean.title: Your app is easy to navigate
navigation_clean.description: Users can find features without getting lost in menus. Nothing is buried behind layers of nesting or hidden behind confusing labels.

upgrade_path_visible.title: Users can see how to upgrade and why
upgrade_path_visible.description: Upgrade options are visible and explain what the user gets. People who want to pay you more can actually find the path to do so.

empty_states_guided.title: New users are not left staring at blank screens
empty_states_guided.description: Empty screens guide the user toward a first action — sample data, suggestions, or clear next steps. No one opens your app and feels lost.

parametric_titles.sensitive_input_abandonment: Users abandon after interacting with {field_kind} input
parametric_titles.surface_oscillation_before_dropoff: Back-and-forth between {surfaceA} and {surfaceB} before dropoff due to unresolved decision friction

inference_effects.upgrade_value_gap: Customers ready to upgrade can't justify the cost because features are listed without outcomes — they stay on the current plan and your ARPU stagnates
inference_effects.referral_path_nonexistent: Happy customers have no easy way to bring others — word-of-mouth happens in conversation but never converts to tracked acquisition
inference_effects.success_story_feedback_loop_broken: Real customer wins happen every day but are never turned into testimonials or case studies — your best marketing asset evaporates unused
inference_effects.tone_shift_across_journey: When each page sounds like a different company, buyers lose trust cumulatively — the inconsistency signals disorganization and makes every claim less credible
inference_effects.mobile_journey_friction_compound: Each individual mobile issue is tolerable but together they create an unbearable experience — tiny taps, slow loads, and layout shifts multiply into total abandonment
inference_effects.trust_gradient_inverted: Trust badges and reassurance pile up on the homepage but vanish on checkout and pricing pages — the gradient is backwards, strongest where buyers already trust and absent where they doubt

actions.default_primary: Review findings and address highest severity issues first.
actions.default_verification: Re-run analysis after changes to confirm resolution.

scale_readiness.block_primary: Do not scale traffic until critical issues are resolved.
scale_readiness.block_fix_checkout: Fix checkout integrity: ensure checkout flow stays on-domain or uses a verified provider.
scale_readiness.block_resolve_trust: Resolve trust boundary issues: reduce off-domain handoffs or verify external providers.
scale_readiness.block_add_policies: Add missing policies: privacy, terms, and refund policies are required for commercial sites.
scale_readiness.block_verify_full: Re-run full analysis after fixes to confirm readiness.
scale_readiness.block_verify_checkout: Verify checkout flow manually before scaling.
scale_readiness.fix_primary: Address high-priority issues before increasing traffic significantly.
scale_readiness.fix_improve_policy: Improve policy coverage to reduce compliance risk.
scale_readiness.fix_install_analytics: Install analytics tools to enable proper attribution and optimization.
scale_readiness.fix_strengthen_revenue: Strengthen revenue path: reduce redirects and friction in the conversion flow.
scale_readiness.optimize_primary: Traffic can be scaled. Monitor for regressions.
scale_readiness.optimize_measurement: Consider improving measurement coverage for better optimization.
scale_readiness.optimize_verify: Schedule periodic re-analysis to detect regressions.

revenue_integrity.block_primary: Revenue is actively leaking. Fix the conversion path immediately.
revenue_integrity.block_close_leak: Close revenue leak points: fix broken forms, consolidate checkout to one domain, and ensure clear conversion entry.
revenue_integrity.block_consolidate: Consolidate conversion path: reduce external host fragmentation and eliminate unnecessary redirects before checkout.
revenue_integrity.block_restore_trust: Restore trust at checkout: add policies, verify providers, ensure brand continuity through the purchase flow.
revenue_integrity.block_reduce_friction: Reduce critical path friction: fix broken form actions, speed up slow pages, eliminate unnecessary redirects.
revenue_integrity.block_verify_revenue: Re-run revenue analysis after fixes to confirm leakage is resolved.
revenue_integrity.block_verify_conversion: Monitor conversion rate after changes to measure improvement.
revenue_integrity.fix_primary: Revenue path has structural issues. Address before scaling ad spend.
revenue_integrity.fix_measurement: Improve measurement: install analytics on commercial pages to make revenue leakage visible.
revenue_integrity.fix_clarify_intent: Clarify conversion intent: establish a clear primary CTA and reduce competing actions.
revenue_integrity.fix_streamline: Streamline checkout flow: minimize handoffs and keep the conversion path on-domain where possible.
revenue_integrity.optimize_primary: Revenue path is functional but has optimization opportunities.
revenue_integrity.optimize_measurement: Improve measurement coverage to better quantify revenue impact.
revenue_integrity.optimize_clarify: Clarify primary conversion path for better conversion rates.
revenue_integrity.optimize_verify: Schedule periodic revenue analysis to track improvements.
revenue_integrity.stable_primary: Revenue integrity is stable. No significant leakage detected.
revenue_integrity.stable_verify: Schedule periodic revenue analysis to detect regressions.

chargeback.block_primary: High chargeback exposure. Add refund policy, support channels, and trust signals immediately.
chargeback.block_refund_policy: Add a clear, accessible refund/return policy. This is the single most effective chargeback prevention measure.
chargeback.block_add_support: Add visible support channels (email, phone, or chat). Customers who can reach support resolve issues without disputes.
chargeback.block_clarify_pricing: Clarify pricing and add order confirmation. Expectation misalignment drives "unauthorized charge" disputes.
chargeback.block_verify_policies: Re-run analysis after adding policies and support channels.
chargeback.block_verify_checkout: Review checkout flow for brand continuity and charge clarity.
chargeback.fix_primary: Moderate chargeback risk. Strengthen policies and support before scaling.
chargeback.fix_refund: Improve refund policy clarity and accessibility.
chargeback.fix_support: Add additional support channels for better customer accessibility.
chargeback.fix_confirmation: Add order confirmation and pricing transparency.
chargeback.optimize_primary: Low chargeback risk. Minor improvements available.
chargeback.optimize_support: Consider adding more support channels for redundancy.
chargeback.optimize_communication: Improve post-purchase communication for better customer experience.
chargeback.strong_primary: Chargeback resilience is strong. Continue monitoring.

security_posture.incident_primary: You have critical security holes that buyers can see. Fix these before taking another payment.
security_posture.incident_sensitive_endpoint: Lock down or remove the admin pages, config files, and backups that are currently public.
security_posture.incident_mixed_content: Stop loading insecure content on your sale pages — every resource needs to come over HTTPS or buyers see broken padlocks.
security_posture.incident_open_redirect: Close the open redirects on your site — attackers can use them to send your customers to fake checkout pages.
security_posture.incident_security_headers: Add the basic security headers (CSP, HSTS, X-Frame-Options) so the browser stops flagging your site as risky.
security_posture.incident_verify_rerun: Run a new audit after the fixes so we can confirm the holes are closed.
security_posture.incident_verify_endpoints: Double-check that the previously exposed pages are no longer reachable from the open internet.
security_posture.fix_primary: Your security has gaps that will hurt you at scale. Close them before pushing more traffic.
security_posture.fix_security_headers: Tighten your security headers so the site is less exposed to injection and clickjacking attacks.
security_posture.fix_open_redirect: Fix the open redirect endpoints — they are easy to abuse.
security_posture.fix_verify_rerun: Run a new audit after the changes to confirm they took effect.
security_posture.optimize_primary: Your security holds up. There are still some hardening wins available.
security_posture.optimize_csp: Consider stricter Content-Security-Policy and Permissions-Policy headers for extra defense.
security_posture.optimize_verify: Re-check security posture every few months to catch regressions.
security_posture.observe_primary: Your security is in good shape. No significant exposures right now.
security_posture.observe_verify: Schedule a periodic security check to keep this clean.

summaries.fix_before_scale: Issues should be fixed before scaling traffic. Risk score: {risk_score}/100, confidence: {confidence_score}/100.
summaries.ready_with_risks: Traffic can be scaled with caution. Some risks remain (score: {risk_score}/100) but are not blocking.
summaries.safe_to_scale: Safe to scale traffic. No significant risks detected (score: {risk_score}/100).
summaries.revenue_leakage_detected: Active revenue leakage detected. Risk score: {risk_score}/100, confidence: {confidence_score}/100. Conversion path has critical structural issues losing money.
summaries.revenue_at_risk: Revenue path has significant issues. Risk score: {risk_score}/100, confidence: {confidence_score}/100. Fix before increasing ad spend.
summaries.revenue_path_fragile: Revenue path is functional but fragile. Risk score: {risk_score}/100. Optimization opportunities exist to reduce leakage.

summaries.high_chargeback_risk: High chargeback risk. Risk score: {risk_score}/100, confidence: {confidence_score}/100. Missing policies, support gaps, and expectation issues create significant dispute exposure.
summaries.moderate_chargeback_risk: Moderate chargeback risk. Risk score: {risk_score}/100, confidence: {confidence_score}/100. Some protective measures are missing.
summaries.low_chargeback_risk: Low chargeback risk. Risk score: {risk_score}/100. Minor improvements available but not blocking.
summaries.chargeback_resilience_strong: Chargeback resilience is strong. No significant dispute risk detected (score: {risk_score}/100).
summaries.payment_health_critical: Payment health is critical. Risk score: {risk_score}/100, confidence: {confidence_score}/100. Failed payment rate or subscriber churn has crossed dangerous thresholds.
summaries.payment_health_at_risk: Payment health is at risk. Risk score: {risk_score}/100, confidence: {confidence_score}/100. Elevated failed payments or subscriber churn are creating preventable revenue loss.
summaries.payment_health_stable: Payment health is stable. Failed payment and churn rates are within acceptable ranges (score: {risk_score}/100).
summaries.copy_misaligned: Copy is misaligned with commercial intent. Risk score: {risk_score}/100, confidence: {confidence_score}/100. Messaging is failing to convert high-intent visitors across multiple dimensions.
summaries.copy_significant_gaps: Copy has significant alignment gaps. Risk score: {risk_score}/100, confidence: {confidence_score}/100. Multiple CRO dimensions need attention before scaling.
summaries.copy_minor_gaps: Copy has minor alignment gaps. Risk score: {risk_score}/100. Messaging is mostly effective but refinements could lift conversion.
summaries.copy_aligned: Copy is aligned with commercial intent. No significant gaps detected (score: {risk_score}/100).
summaries.channel_integrity_critical: Critical channel integrity compromise detected. Risk score: {risk_score}/100, confidence: {confidence_score}/100. Payment surfaces, scripts, or infrastructure are exposed to tampering or hijack.
summaries.channel_integrity_elevated: Elevated channel integrity risk. Risk score: {risk_score}/100, confidence: {confidence_score}/100. Abuse vectors or brittle infrastructure need attention before scaling.
summaries.channel_integrity_weak: Channel integrity has minor gaps. Risk score: {risk_score}/100. Hardening opportunities exist but are not blocking.
summaries.channel_integrity_strong: Channel integrity is strong. No significant compromise vectors detected (score: {risk_score}/100).
summaries.discoverability_critically_weak: Discoverability is critically weak. Risk score: {risk_score}/100, confidence: {confidence_score}/100. Commercial pages are invisible to search engines and social platforms.
summaries.discoverability_gaps_significant: Significant discoverability gaps. Risk score: {risk_score}/100, confidence: {confidence_score}/100. Search representation and social previews need improvement before scaling traffic.
summaries.discoverability_improvable: Discoverability has optimization opportunities. Risk score: {risk_score}/100. Pages are indexable but not well-represented.
summaries.discoverability_adequate: Discoverability is adequate. No significant gaps detected (score: {risk_score}/100).
summaries.brand_integrity_critical: Critical brand integrity threat. Risk score: {risk_score}/100, confidence: {confidence_score}/100. Active impersonation or phishing surfaces are diverting customer trust and revenue.
summaries.brand_integrity_elevated: Elevated brand integrity risk. Risk score: {risk_score}/100, confidence: {confidence_score}/100. Lookalike domains or deceptive surfaces are competing for brand traffic.
summaries.brand_integrity_weak: Brand integrity has minor exposure. Risk score: {risk_score}/100. Brand presence is diluted but not under active threat.
summaries.brand_integrity_strong: Brand integrity is strong. No significant impersonation or dilution detected (score: {risk_score}/100).
summaries.security_posture_critical: Critical security exposures detected. Risk score: {risk_score}/100, confidence: {confidence_score}/100. Sensitive files or mixed content on commercial pages create immediate financial risk.
summaries.security_posture_elevated: Elevated security posture risk. Risk score: {risk_score}/100, confidence: {confidence_score}/100. Weak headers or open redirects increase exposure.
summaries.security_posture_weak: Security posture has minor gaps. Risk score: {risk_score}/100. Hardening opportunities exist but are not blocking.
summaries.security_posture_adequate: Security posture is adequate. No significant exposures detected (score: {risk_score}/100).

confidence_narrative.high_high: Analysis is structurally sound and economic estimates are well-calibrated.
confidence_narrative.high_not_high: Structural analysis is reliable, but economic estimates carry uncertainty. The issues identified are real; the dollar amounts should be treated as directional.
confidence_narrative.not_high_high: Economic data is strong but structural evidence could be stronger. Consider verification to strengthen confidence in the identified issues.
confidence_narrative.low_low: Both structural evidence and economic data carry uncertainty. Findings are directional — verify before taking action on specific dollar estimates.

behavioral_issues.first_session_milestone_stall: First-time visitors are leaving before they show intent to buy
behavioral_issues.first_session_trust_barrier: New visitors hit a trust wall before they engage
behavioral_issues.first_session_cta_timing_gap: It takes too long for new visitors to see what to do
behavioral_issues.low_value_action_dominates: Visitors are spending attention on actions that do not make money
behavioral_issues.high_value_action_underexposed: The actions that make you money are too hard to find
behavioral_issues.dead_weight_surface_traffic: Pages that bring no revenue are eating your traffic
behavioral_issues.paid_traffic_friction_elevated: Visitors from your ads are hitting more friction than the average buyer
behavioral_issues.paid_traffic_trust_gap: Visitors from ads do not trust your site as fast as organic visitors
behavioral_issues.paid_mobile_compounding_waste: Your paid mobile traffic is leaking money on two fronts at once
behavioral_issues.mobile_conversion_gap: Mobile buyers convert way less than desktop buyers
behavioral_issues.mobile_form_friction_elevated: Your forms are painful to fill out on a phone
behavioral_issues.mobile_cta_timing_degraded: Your buy buttons take too long to react on mobile
behavioral_issues.funnel_step_friction_cost: Each step in your funnel is costing you more than it should
behavioral_issues.oscillation_decision_cost: Buyers are bouncing back and forth before deciding — and many never decide

behavioral_issues.trust_deficit_conversion_drag: Buyers want to convert but trust gaps are pulling them away

behavioral_issues.sensitive_input_trust_gap: Buyers are abandoning the moment they have to type their card or personal data

refund_policy.meta_description: Vestigio Refund Policy. Understand how refund requests, cancellations, and billing disputes are handled.
refund_policy.heading: Refund Policy
refund_policy.translation_disclaimer: This is a courtesy English translation of our Portuguese Refund Policy. In the event of any conflict or inconsistency between versions, the Portuguese version prevails.
refund_policy.intro: This Refund Policy governs requests for reversal, refund, financial cancellation, and handling of charges related to the Services of VESTIGIO TECNOLOGIA LTDA.
refund_policy.s1_body: Vestigio offers recurring SaaS services, free trial, paid plans, and AI credits. As a general rule:
s1_items: amounts related to periods already started, used, made available, or effectively enjoyed are not refundable;
what has been used, has been used;
cancellation does not automatically amount to a refund;
AI credits are handled according to specific rules in this Policy.
refund_policy.s2_title: 2. Refund of monthly and annual plans
refund_policy.s2_body_1: The Customer may be eligible for a subscription refund only when all of the following conditions are cumulatively met:
s2_items: there is a material issue, relevant defect, or unavailability attributable to Vestigio itself;
the issue substantially compromises normal use of the contracted Service;
the issue is reported to support at support@vestigio.io with enough detail for analysis;
Vestigio confirms the existence of the issue;
Vestigio is unable to resolve the issue within 72 (seventy-two) hours from the initial support validation;
the Customer cooperates in good faith with the investigation, including by providing information, logs, screenshots, evidence, and, when reasonably necessary, technical means for reproduction.
refund_policy.s2_body_2: Even when eligible, the refund will be limited only to the portion of the subscription not yet enjoyed.
refund_policy.s2_body_3: There will be no refund for the portion of the period already used, made available, or effectively enjoyed by the Customer.
refund_policy.s3_title: 3. Situations in which no refund will be granted
refund_policy.s3_body: No refund will be granted when the issue results, in whole or in part, from:
s3_items: environment, domain, store, account, integration, pixel, or snippet controlled by the Customer or by third parties;
unavailability, limitation, change, or blocking caused by third parties;
outage, failure, or limitation of external integrations, platforms, or APIs;
abusive, fraudulent, or unauthorized use of the platform;
frustrated commercial expectation without an actual Service defect;
subjective interpretation of outputs generated by AI or automation;
attempt to use the platform on assets without adequate authorization.
s4_items: are tied to the paid plan, unless expressly stated otherwise;
may be purchased, accumulated, or consumed according to product rules;
are not refundable, including when unused, except for improper charges or a mandatory legal requirement to the contrary.
refund_policy.s4_body_2: Once made available to the account, AI credits will not be returned in cash, refunded proportionally, or converted into an automatic refund.
refund_policy.s5_body: Free trial periods, promotional releases, promotional credits, free tests, or experimentally granted access do not give rise to a right of refund.
refund_policy.s6_body: Subscription cancellation prevents future charges related to recurring renewal but does not automatically generate a refund of amounts already charged.
refund_policy.s7_body: If there is a proven improper, duplicate, or incorrect charge, Vestigio may, at its discretion and as appropriate:
s7_items: offset the amount in a future cycle;
refund_policy.s8_body: Refund requests or charge disputes must be sent to support@vestigio.io with:
s8_items: proof or reference of the charge;
refund_policy.s9_body_1: When approved, the refund may be processed through the same payment method originally used, as credit, reversal, deduction, or any other operationally viable means, including via the applicable payment processor, such as Paddle.
refund_policy.s9_body_2: Final settlement times may depend on banks, card networks, financial institutions, and third-party processors.
refund_policy.s10_body_1: Before opening a chargeback, bank dispute, or contestation with the payment processor, the Customer must seek an administrative resolution with Vestigio.
refund_policy.s10_body_2: Abusive, improper, or Policy-inconsistent chargebacks may result in account suspension, termination of access, and adoption of applicable measures.
